title: The Path to Set-Theoretic Paradoxes
description: A flowchart illustrating the logical progression from naive set theory concepts to the formulation of famous paradoxes, showing how unrestricted set formation leads to contradictions.
elements: Russell's Paradox (Set of all sets that are not members of themselves)
Cantor's Paradox (Set of all cardinal numbers)
Burali-Forti Paradox (Set of all ordinal numbers)
